title: Drill holder
abstract: A drill holder (100) comprising: a base (108); an elongate support member (110) extending substantially perpendicularly from the base (108); and an arm (112) mounted to the support member (110); wherein the arm (112) extends substantially perpendicularly from the support member (110); the arm (112) is free to slide along at least a portion of the support member (110); and the arm (112) comprises: holding means (126) for holding a drill (102); and a weight (124).
claims: Claims 1 . A drill holder comprising: a base; an elongate support member extending substantially perpendicularly from the base; and an arm mounted to the support member; wherein the arm extends substantially perpendicularly from the support member; the arm is free to slide along at least a portion of the support member; and the arm comprises: holding means for holding a drill; and a weight. 2. The drill holder of claim 1 , wherein: the arm comprises a first end and a second end opposite to the first end; the arm is slidably attached to the support member at the first end; the holding means are located at the second end; and the weight is located between the first end and the second end. 3. The drill holder of claim 1 or 2, wherein: the support member comprises a plurality of first through holes spaced apart along its length; the arm comprises a second through hole configured to align with each of the first through holes when the arm is located at a different respective position along the length of the support member; and the drill holder further comprises a locking pin for inserting through the second through hole and a first through hole aligned therewith. 4. The drill holder of any of claims 1 to 3, wherein the arm further comprises a handle. 5. The drill holder of any of claims 1 to 4, wherein the support member has a length of less than or equal to 2m. 6. The drill holder of any of claims 1 to 5, wherein the weight has a weight of between 0.5kg and 5.0kg. 7. The drill holder of claim 6, wherein the weight has a weight of between 0.5kg and 2.5kg. 8. The drill holder of claim 7, wherein the weight has a weight of between 1.0kg and 2.0kg. 9. The drill holder of claim 8, wherein the weight has a weight of between 1.0kg and 1.5kg. 10. The drill holder of any of claims 1 to 9, wherein the weight has a variable weight. 11. The drill holder of any of claims 1 to 10, further comprising a vacuum system coupled to the base. 12. The drill holder of any of claims 1 to 11 , further comprising attachment means for fixedly attaching the base to a surface to be drilled. 13. The drill holder of any of claims 1 to 12, wherein the base comprises a plurality of lockable wheels. 14. The drill holder of any of claims 1 to 13, wherein the base comprises a guide hole for receiving and guiding a drill bit of a drill held by the holding means. 15. The drill holder of any of claims 1 to 14, wherein the arm further comprises vibration damping means. 16. A system comprising a plurality of drill holders, each of the drill holders being in accordance with any of claims 1 to 15; wherein the bases of the drill holders are integrally formed. 17. A drilling system comprising: a drill holder in accordance with any of claims 1 to 15; and a drill fixedly held by the holding means of the drill holder. 18. The drilling system of claim 17, wherein the drill is selected from a group of drills consisting of a rotary percussion drill, a hammer drill, a rotary hammer drill, a percussive breaker, an electric drill, a pneumatic drill, and a hydraulic drill. 19. The drilling system of claim 17 or 18, further comprising a controller for activating and deactivating the drill, wherein the controller is remote from the drill holder and the drill. 20. The drilling system of any of claims 17 to 19, wherein a weight of the drill is less than or equal to 50kg. 21. The drilling system of any of claims 17 to 20, wherein a weight of the drill is between 5kg and 10kg. 22. A drilling method comprising: providing a drill holder, the drill holder being in accordance with any of claims 1 to 15; fixedly coupling the base to a surface of material to be drilled; holding, by the holding means, a drill; activating the drill; and applying, by the weight, a downwards force to the drill, thereby to force the drill against the surface. 23. The drilling method of claim 22, wherein: prior to the holding of the drill by the holding means, the arm is prevented from moving with respect to the support member by means of a locking pin positioned through a first hole through the arm and a second hole through the support member, the first and second holes being aligned; and the method further comprises, following activation of the drill, removing the locking pin from the first and second holes. 24. The drilling method of claim 22 or 23, wherein activating the drill comprises remotely activating the drill by a controller remote from the drill holder and the drill. 25. The drilling method of any of claims 22 to 24, wherein the material comprises at least one of stone, concrete, brick, or mortar.
